message:
fix(type-inference): Prevent comprehension variables from polluting global scope

Comprehension loop variables (listcomp, dictcomp, genexp) now properly
restore outer scope types after inference completes. Previously, a loop
like `{g: None for g in range(10)}` would permanently change the global
variable `g` to type i64, causing type mismatches.

Changes:
- Save outer variable types before adding comprehension loop variables
- Restore original types (or remove if none) after element type inference
- Applied consistently to listcomp, dictcomp, and genexp

Also adds fallback type inference from assignment values when var_types
lookup fails in global variable pre-declaration.
